channel.title: Publications on Emilie Yu
channel.description: Recent content in Publications on Emilie Yu

item.title: Clay ARtools: Digital Ceramics Tools in AR to Support Fine-Grained Machine Toolpath Editing
item.description: We developed a set of digital tools in an AR system to support gestural application of precise toolpath manipulation effects, which we apply to the creation of clay 3D printing toolpaths.

item.title: StepDance: A Toolkit for Redesigning CNC Machines Using Physical Metaphors
item.description: We contribute a new creative motion control system, StepDance, which consists of two elements: 1) modular controllers that replace the GCode controller of a CNC and can be chained together to develop new interfaces, and 2) a modular programming library that supports declarative mappings between live user input, pre-programmed operations, and machine motion.

item.title: WORM: Programming Collaborative Robots Through Manual Actions for Craft-Aligned Digital Fabrication
item.description: We sought to enable artists to precisely specify domain-specific machining operations to be executed by a cobot (collaborative robot arm). We contributed a novel cobot programming framework that allows artists to directly manipulate the end-effector to define robotic behaviors.

item.title: Computational Craft: Computational Fabrication Methods for Craft Production
item.description: This course introduced Siggraph attendees to foundational concepts and methods in applying computational design and digital fabrication techniques to craft production. We covered methods from ceramics and textiles production, highlighting our own research and other work in these fields, and pointing out future opportunities for graphics researchers to do work on computational craft.

item.title: Towards a sustainable use of GPUs in Graphics Research
item.description: We surveyed 888 SIGGRAPH papers from 2018-2024 and gathered author-reported GPU models. By contextualizing the hardware reported in papers with available data of consumers’ hardware, we demonstrate that research is consistently developed and tested on new high-end devices that do not reflect the state of the consumer-level market.

item.title: texTile: Making and Re-making Crochet Granny Square Garments Through Computational Design and 3D-printed Connectors
item.description: texTile is a modular fashion workflow that enables designers to assemble reusable crochet tiles into garments. To support this workflow, we developed digitally fabricated connectors for easy assembly and disassembly, a custom pattern solver and user interface to guide garment design, and a visualization tool to help plan manual assembly and reassembly.

item.title: 3D-Layers: Bringing Layer-Based Color Editing to VR Painting
item.description: VR artists create beautiful 3D paintings with colored 3D brushstrokes. We investigated how the paradigm of ``painting layers’’ could translate to the 3D painting space to achieve non-destructive color editing effects.

item.title: PhD Thesis: Designing Tools for 3D Content Authoring Based on 3D Sketching
item.description: 3D strokes: what are they? what can we use them for?

item.title: VideoDoodles: Hand-Drawn Animations on Videos with Scene-Aware Canvases
item.description: We studied how artists create VideoDoodles (mix of video and hand-drawn animation) and proposed a novel user interface to simplify this task based on depth and motion priors. TL;DR: computer vision as a scaffold for humans to play with animated doodles.

item.title: Piecewise-Smooth Surface Fitting onto Unstructured 3D Sketches
item.description: We proposed a method to transform unstructured 3D sketches into piecewise smooth surfaces that preserve sketched geometric features.

item.title: CASSIE: Curve And Surface Sketching in Immersive Environments
item.description: We proposed a 3D sketching interface in VR, where the user’s strokes are automatically neatened to form a well-connected curve network. We also infer and create surface patches on the sketch, making it possible to create a 3D surface from a few 3D brush strokes.

item.title: MSc Thesis: Interactive 3D Sketching in Virtual Reality
item.description: During my MSc thesis at DTU supervised by Andreas Bærentzen, Adrien Bousseau and Tibor Stanko, I implemented a VR sketching application in Unity and experimented with automatic neatening of strokes, formation of curve networks from the strokes, and infering surface patches. We finally conducted a small remote user study. This work was the basis for our next paper, CASSIE.
